# Celine Dion albums discography

![This is a featured list. Click here for more information.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/e/e7/Cscr-featured.svg/20px-Cscr-featured.svg.png)

| [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") albums discography | |
| --- | --- |
| Dion performing on the [Taking Chances World Tour](/wiki/Taking_Chances_World_Tour "Taking Chances World Tour") in August 2008 | |
| Studio albums | 27 |
| Soundtrack albums | 2 |
| Live albums | 7 |
| Compilation albums | 20 |

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/42/Celine_Dion_Concert_Singing_Taking_Chances_2008.jpg/250px-Celine_Dion_Concert_Singing_Taking_Chances_2008.jpg)

Canadian singer [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") has released 27 [studio albums](/wiki/Album#Studio "Album"), 7 [live albums](/wiki/Album#Live "Album"), 20 [compilation albums](/wiki/Compilation_album "Compilation album"), and 25 [box sets](/wiki/Box_set "Box set"). Often described as the "Queen of power ballads",[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Dion is the best-selling Canadian artist of all time and the best-selling artist of the [Nielsen SoundScan](/wiki/Nielsen_SoundScan "Nielsen SoundScan") era in Canada (from 1996 to the present).[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) She is also the second best-selling female artist of the Nielsen era in the United States, with over 53.2 million albums sold since Nielsen began tracking sales in 1991.[[6]](#cite_note-6) *[Guinness World Records](/wiki/Guinness_World_Records "Guinness World Records")* recognises her as the Top Selling Album Act in Europe, with 33 million certified album sales since 1996.[[7]](#cite_note-7) In 2003, Dion was honoured by the [International Federation of the Phonographic Industry](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") for selling 50 million albums in Europe.[[8]](#cite_note-8) *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* ranked her the second best-selling female album artist of the 2000s in the United States, with 17.3 million albums sold via SoundScan.[[9]](#cite_note-9) According to *Billboard*, Dion is the sixth greatest [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") female solo artist of all time,[[10]](#cite_note-10) as well as the eighth greatest female solo artist of all time in *Billboard'*s overall chart history.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Her debut album, *[La voix du bon Dieu](/wiki/La_voix_du_bon_Dieu "La voix du bon Dieu")*, was issued in 1981. In the 1980s, Dion released her French-language albums in Canada, with several compilation albums also issued in France. Her first English-language album, *[Unison](/wiki/Unison_(Celine_Dion_album) "Unison (Celine Dion album)")*, was released in 1990 and has sold over four million copies worldwide.[[12]](#cite_note-unison-12) It was followed by *[Dion chante Plamondon](/wiki/Dion_chante_Plamondon "Dion chante Plamondon")* in 1991 and *[Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion_(album) "Celine Dion (album)")* in 1992. The latter became one of six of her albums to be certified [Diamond](/wiki/Music_Canada#Certification_awards "Music Canada") in Canada for shipments of at least one million units.[[13]](#cite_note-cria-13) Dion's popularity became firmly established with her 1993 album, *[The Colour of My Love](/wiki/The_Colour_of_My_Love "The Colour of My Love")*, which topped charts in several countries, including the United Kingdom, Canada, and Australia, and has sold 20 million copies worldwide.[[14]](#cite_note-thecolourofmylove-14) In the United States, it was certified [six-times platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification").[[15]](#cite_note-riaa-15) Released in 1995, *[D'eux](/wiki/D%27eux "D'eux")* became the best-selling French-language album in history, with sales of 12 million copies worldwide.[[16]](#cite_note-elle.fr-16)[[17]](#cite_note-deux-17) In France alone, *D'eux* spent 44 weeks at number one and has sold 4.5 million units, becoming the best-selling album of all time.[[18]](#cite_note-10million-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19) It also became Dion's first of six [Diamond](/wiki/Syndicat_National_de_l%27%C3%89dition_Phonographique#Certification_awards "Syndicat National de l'Édition Phonographique")-certified albums in France.[[20]](#cite_note-snep-20)

Canadian singer [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") has released 27 [studio albums](/wiki/Album#Studio "Album"), 2 [soundtrack albums](/wiki/Soundtrack_album "Soundtrack album"), 7 [live albums](/wiki/Album#Live "Album"), 20 [compilation albums](/wiki/Compilation_album "Compilation album"), and 25 [box sets](/wiki/Box_set "Box set"). Often described as the "Queen of power ballads",[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Dion is the best-selling Canadian artist of all time and the best-selling artist of the [Nielsen SoundScan](/wiki/Nielsen_SoundScan "Nielsen SoundScan") era in Canada (from 1996 to the present).[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) She is also the second best-selling female artist of the Nielsen era in the United States, with over 53.2 million albums sold since Nielsen began tracking sales in 1991.[[6]](#cite_note-6) *[Guinness World Records](/wiki/Guinness_World_Records "Guinness World Records")* recognises her as the Top Selling Album Act in Europe, with 33 million certified album sales since 1996.[[7]](#cite_note-7) In 2003, Dion was honoured by the [International Federation of the Phonographic Industry](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") for selling 50 million albums in Europe.[[8]](#cite_note-8) *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* ranked her the second best-selling female album artist of the 2000s in the United States, with 17.3 million albums sold via SoundScan.[[9]](#cite_note-9) According to *Billboard*, Dion is the sixth greatest [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") female solo artist of all time,[[10]](#cite_note-10) as well as the eighth greatest female solo artist of all time in *Billboard'*s overall chart history.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Her debut album, *[La voix du bon Dieu](/wiki/La_voix_du_bon_Dieu "La voix du bon Dieu")*, was issued in 1981. In the 1980s, Dion released her French-language albums in Canada, with several compilation albums also issued in France. Her first English-language album, *[Unison](/wiki/Unison_(Celine_Dion_album) "Unison (Celine Dion album)")*, was released in 1990 and has sold over four million copies worldwide.[[12]](#cite_note-unison-12) It was followed by *[Dion chante Plamondon](/wiki/Dion_chante_Plamondon "Dion chante Plamondon")* in 1991 and *[Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion_(album) "Celine Dion (album)")* in 1992. ***Courage*** is the twenty-seventh studio album and twelfth English-language album by Canadian singer [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion"). Released by [Columbia Records](/wiki/Columbia_Records "Columbia Records") on 15 November 2019, it was created with a diverse group of writers and producers, including [Sia](/wiki/Sia "Sia"), [Sam Smith](/wiki/Sam_Smith "Sam Smith"), [David Guetta](/wiki/David_Guetta "David Guetta"), [Lauv](/wiki/Lauv "Lauv"), [LP](/wiki/LP_(singer) "LP (singer)"), [Greg Kurstin](/wiki/Greg_Kurstin "Greg Kurstin"), [StarGate](/wiki/Stargate_(production_team) "Stargate (production team)"), [Jimmy Napes](/wiki/Jimmy_Napes "Jimmy Napes"), [Jörgen Elofsson](/wiki/J%C3%B6rgen_Elofsson "Jörgen Elofsson"), [Stephan Moccio](/wiki/Stephan_Moccio "Stephan Moccio"), [Eg White](/wiki/Eg_White "Eg White"), and Liz Rodrigues of [The New Royales](/wiki/The_New_Royales "The New Royales").

The album marked Dion's return to the studio following the end of her Las Vegas residency, [Celine](/wiki/Celine_(concert_residency) "Celine (concert residency)"), in June 2019. She released "[Flying on My Own](/wiki/Flying_on_My_Own "Flying on My Own")" as a gift to fans shortly afterward, and in September 2019 launched the [Courage World Tour](/wiki/Courage_World_Tour "Courage World Tour"), where she premiered three additional songs: "[Imperfections](/wiki/Imperfections_(song) "Imperfections (song)")", "[Lying Down](/wiki/Lying_Down "Lying Down")", and "[Courage](/wiki/Courage_(Celine_Dion_song) "Courage (Celine Dion song)")". *Courage* includes 16 tracks, with four more on the deluxe edition, and received generally favourable reviews from music critics.

The album debuted atop the US [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), becoming Dion's first number-one album there in 17 years. It also entered at number one in Canada, Belgium, and Switzerland, and reached the top 10 in numerous other countries, including number two in the United Kingdom, France, and Australia, number four in Germany, Austria, and Croatia, and number five in Ireland, New Zealand, and Portugal.

## Étapes détaillées

### 1. Départ — Centre de Chambéry (Place du Centenaire)
- Depuis la **Place du Centenaire**, se diriger vers la **Rue du Vieux Cimetière** (direction nord-ouest)
- Tourner à gauche dans la **Rue du Vieux Cimetière** (95 m)
- Tourner à gauche dans la **Rue Jean Péru**, puis continuer sur la **Rue Marie de Solms**

### 2. Rejoindre la sortie est de Chambéry
- Suivre les panneaux **« Grenoble / Lyon / Montmélian »**
- Rejoindre le **Boulevard de la Colonne** ou le **Boulevard du Théâtre**, direction **A43 / A41**

### 3. Prendre l'A43 en direction de Grenoble / Montmélian
- Emprunter l'**A43** (autoroute de la Maurienne) vers l'est *(direction Grenoble / Albertville)*
- Ou emprunter la **D1006** (ancienne RN 6) le long de la Combe de Savoie si vous souhaitez éviter l'autoroute

### 4. Sortie vers Puygros
- Prendre la **sortie Montmélian / Saint-Pierre-d'Albigny** (A43)
- Suivre la **D1006** ou les panneaux locaux vers **Puygros**
- Puygros est accessible par la **D201** depuis la D1006 (bifurcation bien indiquée)

### 5. Arrivée — Puygros (73190)
- Entrée dans le village de **Puygros**, commune de la Combe de Savoie

---

## Récapitulatif

| Étape | Route | Distance approx. |
|---|---|---|
| Chambéry centre → bretelle A43 | Rues du centre-ville | ~2 km |
| A43 direction Grenoble | Autoroute A43 | ~8 km |
| Sortie Montmélian → Puygros | D1006 + D201 | ~4,4 km |
| **Total** | | **14,4 km** |

---

## 💡 Conseils pratiques
- **Péage :** L'A43 est gratuite sur ce tronçon court entre Chambéry et Montmélian.
- **Alternative sans autoroute :** Suivre la **D1006** depuis Chambéry jusqu'à Puygros (légèrement plus long en temps mais panoramique, longeant l'Isère en Combe de Savoie).

  - ``invalid_args`` (tool validation): retry ``gmail_search`` WITH a filter this time; if the error persists, ``done(status="failed", ui_payload=null, result_summary="Je n'ai pas su construire la recherche Gmail.")``.

## Sommaire

# Discographie de Céline Dion

![Description de cette image, également commentée ci-après](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a3/Celine_Dion_en_concierto.jpg/250px-Celine_Dion_en_concierto.jpg)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [Albums studio](/wiki/Album_studio "Album studio")[↙](#Albums_studio) | 27 |
| [Albums live](/wiki/Album_live "Album live")[↙](#Albums_live) | 7 |
| [Compilations](/wiki/Compilation_(musique) "Compilation (musique)")[↙](#Compilations) | 19 |
| [Singles](/wiki/Single_(musique) "Single (musique)")[↙](#Singles) | 126 |
| [Musiques de film](/wiki/Musique_de_film "Musique de film")[↙](#Musiques_de_film) | 5 |

La **discographie de [Céline Dion](/wiki/C%C3%A9line_Dion "Céline Dion")** comprend l'ensemble des disques produits et publiés au cours de sa carrière. Elle se compose de 27 [albums studio](/wiki/Album_studio "Album studio") (dont 3 ayant pour thème les [chants de Noël](/wiki/Chant_de_No%C3%ABl "Chant de Noël")), 7 [albums en concert](/wiki/Albums_live "Albums live"), 14 [compilations](/wiki/Compilation_(musique) "Compilation (musique)") et près de 130 [singles](/wiki/Single_(musique) "Single (musique)"). Menant une carrière [francophone](/wiki/Francophonie "Francophonie") et [anglophone](/wiki/Anglais "Anglais"), la chanteuse [canadienne](/wiki/Canada "Canada") a publié 15 albums studio en [français](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ais "Français") (sa langue maternelle) et 12 en anglais.

Le premier album studio de Céline Dion, *[La Voix du Bon Dieu](/wiki/La_Voix_du_Bon_Dieu "La Voix du Bon Dieu")*, sort en [1981](/wiki/1981_en_musique "1981 en musique") et est distribué uniquement au [Québec](/wiki/Qu%C3%A9bec "Québec"). Son premier album en anglais, *[Unison](/wiki/Unison_(album) "Unison (album)")*, sort en [1990](/wiki/1990_en_musique "1990 en musique"). À partir des [années 1990](/wiki/Ann%C3%A9es_1990 "Années 1990"), elle alterne les albums et singles en français et en anglais, et parvient à s'imposer comme l'une des plus importantes vendeuses de disques au monde. Ses albums *[Falling into You](/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")* ([1996](/wiki/1996_en_musique "1996 en musique")) et *[Let's Talk About Love](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love_(album_de_C%C3%A9line_Dion) "Let's Talk About Love (album de Céline Dion)")* ([1997](/wiki/1997_en_musique "1997 en musique")) — sur lequel se trouve son plus grand succès commercial et radiophonique, *[My Heart Will Go On](/wiki/My_Heart_Will_Go_On "My Heart Will Go On")*, chanson du film *[Titanic](/wiki/Titanic_(film,_1997) "Titanic (film, 1997)")* sorti la même année — figurent notamment parmi [les plus vendus de l'histoire](/wiki/Liste_des_albums_musicaux_les_plus_vendus "Liste des albums musicaux les plus vendus"), tandis que *[D'eux](/wiki/D%27eux "D'eux")* ([1995](/wiki/1995_en_musique "1995 en musique")) demeure l'album francophone le plus vendu de tous les temps, en [France](/wiki/France "France") et dans le monde.

Les ventes de Céline Dion sont estimées à 230 millions de disques dans le monde[[1]](#cite_note-1),[[2]](#cite_note-2), dont plus de 25 millions en France[[3]](#cite_note-3).

## Sommaire

# Discographie de Céline Dion

![Description de cette image, également commentée ci-après](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a3/Celine_Dion_en_concierto.jpg/250px-Celine_Dion_en_concierto.jpg)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [Albums studio](/wiki/Album_studio "Album studio")[↙](#Albums_studio) | 27 |
| [Albums live](/wiki/Album_live "Album live")[↙](#Albums_live) | 7 |
| [Compilations](/wiki/Compilation_(musique) "Compilation (musique)")[↙](#Compilations) | 19 |
| [Singles](/wiki/Single_(musique) "Single (musique)")[↙](#Singles) | 126 |
| [Musiques de film](/wiki/Musique_de_film "Musique de film")[↙](#Musiques_de_film) | 5 |

La **discographie de [Céline Dion](/wiki/C%C3%A9line_Dion "Céline Dion")** comprend l'ensemble des disques produits et publiés au cours de sa carrière. Elle se compose de 27 [albums studio](/wiki/Album_studio "Album studio") (dont 3 ayant pour thème les [chants de Noël](/wiki/Chant_de_No%C3%ABl "Chant de Noël")), 7 [albums en concert](/wiki/Albums_live "Albums live"), 14 [compilations](/wiki/Compilation_(musique) "Compilation (musique)") et près de 130 [singles](/wiki/Single_(musique) "Single (musique)"). Menant une carrière [francophone](/wiki/Francophonie "Francophonie") et [anglophone](/wiki/Anglais "Anglais"), la chanteuse [canadienne](/wiki/Canada "Canada") a publié 15 albums studio en [français](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ais "Français") (sa langue maternelle) et 12 en anglais.

Le premier album studio de Céline Dion, *[La Voix du Bon Dieu](/wiki/La_Voix_du_Bon_Dieu "La Voix du Bon Dieu")*, sort en [1981](/wiki/1981_en_musique "1981 en musique") et est distribué uniquement au [Québec](/wiki/Qu%C3%A9bec "Québec"). Son premier album en anglais, *[Unison](/wiki/Unison_(album) "Unison (album)")*, sort en [1990](/wiki/1990_en_musique "1990 en musique"). À partir des [années 1990](/wiki/Ann%C3%A9es_1990 "Années 1990"), elle alterne les albums et singles en français et en anglais, et parvient à s'imposer comme l'une des plus importantes vendeuses de disques au monde. Ses albums *[Falling into You](/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")* ([1996](/wiki/1996_en_musique "1996 en musique")) et *[Let's Talk About Love](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love_(album_de_C%C3%A9line_Dion) "Let's Talk About Love (album de Céline Dion)")* ([1997](/wiki/1997_en_musique "1997 en musique")) — sur lequel se trouve son plus grand succès commercial et radiophonique, *[My Heart Will Go On](/wiki/My_Heart_Will_Go_On "My Heart Will Go On")*, chanson du film *[Titanic](/wiki/Titanic_(film,_1997) "Titanic (film, 1997)")* sorti la même année — figurent notamment parmi [les plus vendus de l'histoire](/wiki/Liste_des_albums_musicaux_les_plus_vendus "Liste des albums musicaux les plus vendus"), tandis que *[D'eux](/wiki/D%27eux "D'eux")* ([1995](/wiki/1995_en_musique "1995 en musique")) demeure l'album francophone le plus vendu de tous les temps, en [France](/wiki/France "France") et dans le monde.

Les ventes de Céline Dion sont estimées à 230 millions de disques dans le monde[[1]](#cite_note-1),[[2]](#cite_note-2), dont plus de 25 millions en France[[3]](#cite_note-3).

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f5/C%C3%A9line_Dion_2012.jpg/250px-C%C3%A9line_Dion_2012.jpg)

**Céline Marie Claudette Dion**[[a]](#cite_note-3) (born 30 March 1968) is a Canadian singer, entrepreneur and philanthropist.[[3]](#cite_note-4)[[4]](#cite_note-5) Dubbed the "[Queen of Power Ballads](/wiki/Honorific_nicknames_in_popular_music "Honorific nicknames in popular music")", she is known for her powerful, technically skilled vocals and commercially successful works, which have had a [significant impact on popular music](/wiki/Cultural_impact_of_Celine_Dion "Cultural impact of Celine Dion"). With over 200 million records sold worldwide, Dion is the best-selling Canadian recording artist, the best-selling French-language artist, and one of the [best-selling musical artists](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_music_artists "List of best-selling music artists").

Born into a large family in [Charlemagne, Quebec](/wiki/Charlemagne,_Quebec "Charlemagne, Quebec"), Dion was discovered by her future manager and husband, [René Angélil](/wiki/Ren%C3%A9_Ang%C3%A9lil "René Angélil"), and emerged as a teen star in her home country with eight French-language albums during the 1980s. She gained international recognition by winning the [Eurovision Song Contest 1988](/wiki/Eurovision_Song_Contest_1988 "Eurovision Song Contest 1988"), where she [represented Switzerland](/wiki/Switzerland_in_the_Eurovision_Song_Contest_1988 "Switzerland in the Eurovision Song Contest 1988") with the song "[Ne partez pas sans moi](/wiki/Ne_partez_pas_sans_moi "Ne partez pas sans moi")". Dion went on to release [twelve English-language albums](/wiki/Celine_Dion_albums_discography "Celine Dion albums discography"), including the [best-selling albums](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_albums "List of best-selling albums") *[The Colour of My Love](/wiki/The_Colour_of_My_Love "The Colour of My Love")* (1993), *[Falling into You](/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")* (1996), and *[Let's Talk About Love](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love "Let's Talk About Love")* (1997), as well as *[A New Day Has Come](/wiki/A_New_Day_Has_Come "A New Day Has Come")* (2002), her [21st-century bestseller](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_albums_of_the_21st_century "List of best-selling albums of the 21st century"). She also became known for performing cinematic [theme songs](/wiki/Theme_music "Theme music"), such as "[Beauty and the Beast](/wiki/Beauty_and_the_Beast_(Disney_song) "Beauty and the Beast (Disney song)")" for the [Disney](/wiki/The_Walt_Disney_Company "The Walt Disney Company") [film of the same name](/wiki/Beauty_and_the_Beast_(1991_film) "Beauty and the Beast (1991 film)") (1991), "[Because You Loved Me](/wiki/Because_You_Loved_Me "Because You Loved Me")" for *[Up Close & Personal](/wiki/Up_Close_%26_Personal_(film) "Up Close & Personal (film)")* (1996), and "[My Heart Will Go On](/wiki/My_Heart_Will_Go_On "My Heart Will Go On")" for *[Titanic](/wiki/Titanic_(1997_film) "Titanic (1997 film)")* (1997), which became one of the [best-selling singles](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_singles "List of best-selling singles").

Dion continued releasing French-language albums between each English record, with *[D'eux](/wiki/D%27eux "D'eux")* (1995) becoming the best-selling French-language album. She also built her reputation as [a live performer](/wiki/List_of_Celine_Dion_concerts "List of Celine Dion concerts"), with the [Let's Talk About Love World Tour](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love_World_Tour "Let's Talk About Love World Tour") (1998–1999) and the [Taking Chances World Tour](/wiki/Taking_Chances_World_Tour "Taking Chances World Tour") (2008–2009)—which rank among the [highest-grossing concert tours](/wiki/List_of_highest-grossing_concert_tours "…", "truncated": true}}

# Celine Dion albums discography

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/42/Celine_Dion_Concert_Singing_Taking_Chances_2008.jpg/250px-Celine_Dion_Concert_Singing_Taking_Chances_2008.jpg)

Canadian singer [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") has released 27 [studio albums](/wiki/Album#Studio "Album"), 7 [live albums](/wiki/Album#Live "Album"), 20 [compilation albums](/wiki/Compilation_album "Compilation album"), and 25 [box sets](/wiki/Box_set "Box set"). Often described as the "Queen of power ballads",[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Dion is the best-selling Canadian artist of all time and the best-selling artist of the [Nielsen SoundScan](/wiki/Nielsen_SoundScan "Nielsen SoundScan") era in Canada (from 1996 to the present).[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) She is also the second best-selling female artist of the Nielsen era in the United States, with over 53.2 million albums sold since Nielsen began tracking sales in 1991.[[6]](#cite_note-6) *[Guinness World Records](/wiki/Guinness_World_Records "Guinness World Records")* recognises her as the Top Selling Album Act in Europe, with 33 million certified album sales since 1996.[[7]](#cite_note-7) In 2003, Dion was honoured by the [International Federation of the Phonographic Industry](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") for selling 50 million albums in Europe.[[8]](#cite_note-8) *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* ranked her the second best-selling female album artist of the 2000s in the United States, with 17.3 million albums sold via SoundScan.[[9]](#cite_note-9) According to *Billboard*, Dion is the sixth greatest [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") female solo artist of all time,[[10]](#cite_note-10) as well as the eighth greatest female solo artist of all time in *Billboard'*s overall chart history.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Her debut album, *[La voix du bon Dieu](/wiki/La_voix_du_bon_Dieu "La voix du bon Dieu")*, was issued in 1981. In the 1980s, Dion released her French-language albums in Canada, with several compilation albums also issued in France. Her first English-language album, *[Unison](/wiki/Unison_(Celine_Dion_album) "Unison (Celine Dion album)")*, was released in 1990 and has sold over four million copies worldwide.[[12]](#cite_note-unison-12) It was followed by *[Dion chante Plamondon](/wiki/Dion_chante_Plamondon "Dion chante Plamondon")* in 1991 and *[Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion_(album) "Celine Dion (album)")* in 1992. The latter became one of six of her albums to be certified [Diamond](/wiki/Music_Canada#Certification_awards "Music Canada") in Canada for shipments of at least one million units.[[13]](#cite_note-cria-13) Dion's popularity became firmly established with her 1993 album, *[The Colour of My Love](/wiki/The_Colour_of_My_Love "The Colour of My Love")*, which topped charts in several countries, including the United Kingdom, Canada, and Australia, and has sold 20 million copies worldwide.[[14]](#cite_note-thecolourofmylove-14) In the United States, it was certified [six-times platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification").[[15]](#cite_note-riaa-15) Released in 1995, *[D'eux](/wiki/D%27eux "D'eux")* became the best-selling French-language album in history, with sales of 12 million copies worldwide.[[16]](#cite_note-elle.fr-16)[[17]](#cite_note-deux-17) In France alone, *D'eux* spent 44 weeks at number one and has sold 4.5 million units, becoming the best-selling album of all time.[[18]](#cite_note-10million-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19) It also became Dion's first of six [Diamond](/wiki/Syndicat_National_de_l%27%C3%89dition_Phonographique#Certification_awards "Syndicat National de l'Édition Phonographique")-certified albums in France.[[20]](#cite_note-snep-20)

*[Falling into You](/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")* (1996) and *[Let's Talk About Love](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love "Let's Talk About Love")* (1997) were major successes for Dion, both reaching number one in numerous countries and becoming two of the [best-selling albums of all time](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_albums "List of best-selling albums"), with sales of over 32 million and 31 million copies, respectively.[[21]](#cite_note-fallingintoyou-21)[[22]](#cite_note-letstalkaboutlove-22) Both albums were certified [Diamond](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") in the United States, with *Falling into You* later certified [twelve-times platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") and *Let's Talk About Love* certified eleven-times platinum for 11 million units.[[15]](#cite_note-riaa-15) *Let's Talk About Love* became the first album to be certified [ten-times platinum](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry#Record_sales_certification "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") by the [IFPI](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") for sales of over 10 million copies in Europe.[[23]](#cite_note-europeancerts-23) In 1998, Dion released another French-language album, *[S'il suffisait d'aimer](/wiki/S%27il_suffisait_d%27aimer "S'il suffisait d'aimer")*, and her …", "truncated": true}}

Her debut album, *[La voix du bon Dieu](/wiki/La_voix_du_bon_Dieu "La voix du bon Dieu")*, was issued in 1981. In the 1980s, Dion released her French-language albums in Canada, with several compilation albums also issued in France. Her first English-language album, *[Unison](/wiki/Unison_(Celine_Dion_album) "Unison (Celine Dion album)")*, was released in 1990 and has sold over four million copies worldwide.[[12]](#cite_note-unison-12) It was followed by *[Dion chante Plamondon](/wiki/Dion_chante_Plamondon "Dion chante Plamondon")* in 1991 and *[Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion_(album) "Celine Dion (album)")* in 1992. The latter became one of six of her albums to be certified [Diamond](/wiki/Music_Canada#Certification_awards "Music Canada") in Canada for shipments of at least one million units.[[13]](#cite_note-cria-13) Dion's popularity became firmly established with her 1993 album, *[The Colour of My Love](/wiki/The_Colour_of_My_Love "The Colour of My Love")*, which topped charts in several countries, including the United Kingdom, Canada, and Australia, and has sold 20 million copies worldwide.[[14]](#cite_note-thecolourofmylove-14) In the United States, it was certified [six-times platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification").[[15]](#cite_note-riaa-15) Released in 1995, *[D'eux](/wiki/D%27eux "D'eux")* became the best-selling French-language album in history, with sales of 12 million copies worldwide.[[16]](#cite_note-elle.fr-16)[[17]](#cite_note-deux-17) In France alone, *D'eux* spent 44 weeks at number one and has sold 4.5 million units, becoming the best-selling album of all time.[[18]](#cite_note-10million-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19) It also became Dion's first of six [Diamond](/wiki/Syndicat_National_de_l%27%C3%89dition_Phonographique#Certification_awards "Syndicat National de l'Édition Phonographique")-certified albums in France.[[20]](#cite_note-snep-20)

*[Falling into You](/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")* (1996) and *[Let's Talk About Love](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love "Let's Talk About Love")* (1997) were major successes for Dion, both reaching number one in numerous countries and becoming two of the [best-selling albums of all time](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_albums "List of best-selling albums"), with sales of over 32 million and 31 million copies, respectively.[[21]](#cite_note-fallingintoyou-21)[[22]](#cite_note-letstalkaboutlove-22) Both albums were certified [Diamond](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") in the United States, with *Falling into You* later certified [twelve-times platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") and *Let's Talk About Love* certified eleven-times platinum for 11 million units.[[15]](#cite_note-riaa-15) *Let's Talk About Love* became the first album to be certified [ten-times platinum](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry#Record_sales_certification "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") by the [IFPI](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") for sales of over 10 million copies in Europe.[[23]](#cite_note-europeancerts-23) In 1998, Dion released another French-language album, *[S'il suff…", "truncated": true}}

***Courage*** is the twenty-seventh studio album and twelfth English-language album by Canadian singer [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion"). Released by [Columbia Records](/wiki/Columbia_Records "Columbia Records") on 15 November 2019, it was created with a diverse group of writers and producers, including [Sia](/wiki/Sia "Sia"), [Sam Smith](/wiki/Sam_Smith "Sam Smith"), [David Guetta](/wiki/David_Guetta "David Guetta"), [Lauv](/wiki/Lauv "Lauv"), [LP](/wiki/LP_(singer) "LP (singer)"), [Greg Kurstin](/wiki/Greg_Kurstin "Greg Kurstin"), [StarGate](/wiki/Stargate_(production_team) "Stargate (production team)"), [Jimmy Napes](/wiki/Jimmy_Napes "Jimmy Napes"), [Jörgen Elofsson](/wiki/J%C3%B6rgen_Elofsson "Jörgen Elofsson"), [Stephan Moccio](/wiki/Stephan_Moccio "Stephan Moccio"), [Eg White](/wiki/Eg_White "Eg White"), and Liz Rodrigues of [The New Royales](/wiki/The_New_Royales "The New Royales").

The album marked Dion's return to the studio following the end of her Las Vegas residency, [Celine](/wiki/Celine_(concert_residency) "Celine (concert residency)"), in June 2019. She released "[Flying on My Own](/wiki/Flying_on_My_Own "Flying on My Own")" as a gift to fans shortly afterward, and in September 2019 launched the [Courage World Tour](/wiki/Courage_World_Tour "Courage World Tour"), where she premiered three additional songs: "[Imperfections](/wiki/Imperfections_(song) "Imperfections (song)")", "[Lying Down](/wiki/Lying_Down "Lying Down")", and "[Courage](/wiki/Courage_(Celine_Dion_song) "Courage (Celine Dion song)")". *Courage* includes 16 tracks, with four more on the deluxe edition, and received generally favourable reviews from music critics.

The album debuted atop the US [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), becoming Dion's first number-one album there in 17 years. It also entered at number one in Canada, Belgium, and Switzerland, and reached the top 10 in numerous other countries, including number two in the United Kingdom, France, and Australia, number four in Germany, Austria, and Croatia, and number five in Ireland, New Zealand, and Portugal.

## Background and development

In November 2013, Dion released her previous English-language studio album, *[Loved Me Back to Life](/wiki/Loved_Me_Back_to_Life "Loved Me Back to Life")*.[[1]](#cite_note…", "truncated": true}}

# *Love Again* (soundtrack)

***Love Again (Soundtrack from the Motion Picture)*** is the soundtrack album to the 2023 American romantic comedy‑drama film *[Love Again](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Love_Again_(2023_film) "Love Again (2023 film)")*, directed by [James C. Strouse](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_C._Strouse "James C. Strouse"). It was released by [Columbia Records](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Columbia_Records "Columbia Records") on 12 May 2023. The album includes five new songs by [Celine Dion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") alongside six of her earlier hits. The lead single, "[Love Again](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Love_Again_(Celine_Dion_song) "Love Again (Celine Dion song)")", was issued on 13 April 2023, followed by "[I'll Be](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/I'll_Be_(Celine_Dion_song) "I'll Be (Celine Dion song)")" on 5 May 2023. Other new tracks are "Waiting on You", "Love of My Life", and "[The Gift](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Gift_(Celine_Dion_song) "The Gift (Celine Dion song)")". *Love Again* received positive reviews from music critics and debuted at number one in Quebec, number two on the UK Soundtrack Albums chart, number six in Switzerland's Romandy region and number 10 in Belgium's Wallonia.

## Contents

## Development

In October 2020, [Priyanka Chopra](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Priyanka_Chopra "Priyanka Chopra"), [Sam Heughan](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sam_Heughan "Sam Heughan"), and [Celine Dion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") joined the cast of the film, then tentatively titled *Text for You*.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Principal photography began in October 2020 and concluded in early 2021.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In April 2022, it was announced that the film had been retitled *It's All Coming Back to Me*, after the song "[It's All Coming Back to Me Now](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/It's_All_Coming_Back_to_Me_Now#Celine_Dion_version "It's All Coming Back to Me Now")", which Dion recorded for her 1996 album *[Falling into You](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")*.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In November 2022, the title was changed again to *Love Again*.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The film was released theatrically on 5 May 2023.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Songs

*Love Again* includes 11 songs by [Celine Dion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") that are woven into the narrative of the film and its characters.[[6]](#cite_note-hollywoodreporter-6) The album contains five new Dion songs and six of her past hits, among them a new edit of "[It's All Coming Back to Me Now](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/It's_All_Coming_Back_to_Me_Now#Celine_Dion_version "It's All Coming Back to Me Now")", "[All by Myself](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/All_by_Myself#Celine_Dion_version "All by Myself")", "[That's the Way It Is](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/That's_the_Way_It_Is_(Celine_Dion_song) "That's the Way It Is (Celine Dion song)")", "[Where Does My Heart Beat Now](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Where_Does_My_Heart_Beat_Now "Where Does My Heart Beat Now")", "[Courage](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Courage_(Celine_Dion_song) "Courage (Celine Dion song)")", and the album version of "[A New Day Has Come](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/A_New_Day_Has_Come_(song) "A New Day Has Come (song)")". The soundtrack also includes three score selections written and produced by American film composer [Keegan DeWitt](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Keegan_DeWitt "Keegan DeWitt").[[6]](#cite_note-hollywoodreporter-6)

# *I Am: Celine Dion* (soundtrack)

***I Am: Celine Dion*** is the soundtrack album to the 2024 documentary film *[I Am: Celine Dion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/I_Am:_Celine_Dion "I Am: Celine Dion")*, directed by [Irene Taylor](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Irene_Taylor_(filmmaker) "Irene Taylor (filmmaker)"), which focuses on Canadian singer [Celine Dion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion"). It was released by [Columbia Records](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Columbia_Records "Columbia Records") on 21 June 2024. The album contains 13 of Dion's well‑known songs, along with seven original score tracks composed mainly by [Redi Hasa](https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Redi%20Hasa "it:Redi Hasa") and produced by Alberto Fabris. In Francophone markets, it was issued under the title ***Je suis: Céline Dion***. The album topped the chart in Quebec for three weeks and reached the top 10 on compilation charts in the United Kingdom and Ireland.

## Contents

## Development

[Sony Music](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sony_Music "Sony Music") announced in September 2021 that a [Celine Dion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") feature-length [documentary film](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Documentary_film "Documentary film") was in development.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The official title, *I Am: Celine Dion*, was revealed in January 2024, with [Amazon MGM Studios](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amazon_MGM_Studios "Amazon MGM Studios") acquiring the distribution rights.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The film was directed by [Emmy](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Emmy_Awards "Emmy Awards")-winning and [Academy Award](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards")-nominated filmmaker [Irene Taylor](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Irene_Taylor_(filmmaker) "Irene Taylor (filmmaker)") and focuses on Dion.[[3]](#cite_note-3) It explores her life, career, and her experience living with [stiff-person syndrome](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stiff-person_syndrome "Stiff-person syndrome"). *I Am: Celine Dion* became available for streaming on [Amazon Prime Video](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amazon_Prime_Video "Amazon Prime Video") starting 25 June 2024.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Songs

The soundtrack contains 20 tracks: 13 of Dion's well‑known songs and seven original score pieces mainly composed by Albanian cellist and composer [Redi Hasa](https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Redi%20Hasa "it:Redi Hasa") and produced by Italian bassist and producer Alberto Fabris. The score cue "The Diagnosis" was composed by pianist Ekland Hasa. The English‑language songs include "[My Heart Will Go On](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/My_Heart_Will_Go_On "My Heart Will Go On")", "[Because You Loved Me](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Because_You_Loved_Me "Because You Loved Me")", "[The Power of Love](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Power_of_Love_(Jennifer_Rush_song)#Celine_Dion_version "The Power of Love (Jennifer Rush song)")", "[All by Myself](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/All_by_Myself#Celine_Dion_version "All by Myself")", "[I'm Alive](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/I'm_Alive_(Celine_Dion_song) "I'm Alive (Celine Dion song)")", "[A New Day Has Come](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/A_New_Day_Has_Come_(song) "A New Day Has Come (song)")", and "[River Deep, Mountain High](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Deep_–_Mountain_High "River Deep – Mountain High")". "[Ashes](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ashes_(Celine_Dion_song) "Ashes (Celine Dion song)")" from the [*Deadpool 2* soundtrack](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deadpool_2_(soundtrack) "Deadpool 2 (soundtrack)") (2018) and "[Love Again](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Love_Again_(Celine_Dion_song) "Love Again (Celine Dion song)")" from the [*Love Again* soundtrack](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Love_Again_(soundtrack) "Love Again (soundtrack)") (2023) are also included. The French‑language songs are "[Pour que tu m'aimes encore](//en.wik…", "truncated": true}}

**Céline Marie Claudette Dion**[[a]](#cite_note-3) (born 30 March 1968) is a Canadian singer, entrepreneur and philanthropist.[[3]](#cite_note-4)[[4]](#cite_note-5) Dubbed the "[Queen of Power Ballads](/wiki/Honorific_nicknames_in_popular_music "Honorific nicknames in popular music")", she is known for her powerful, technically skilled vocals and commercially successful works, which have had a [significant impact on popular music](/wiki/Cultural_impact_of_Celine_Dion "Cultural impact of Celine Dion"). With over 200 million records sold worldwide, Dion is the best-selling Canadian recording artist, the best-selling French-language artist, and one of the [best-selling musical artists](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_music_artists "List of best-selling music artists").

Born into a large family in [Charlemagne, Quebec](/wiki/Charlemagne,_Quebec "Charlemagne, Quebec"), Dion was discovered by her future manager and husband, [René Angélil](/wiki/Ren%C3%A9_Ang%C3%A9lil "René Angélil"), and emerged as a teen star in her home country with eight French-language albums during the 1980s. She gained international recognition by winning the [Eurovision Song Contest 1988](/wiki/Eurovision_Song_Contest_1988 "Eurovision Song Contest 1988"), where she [represented Switzerland](/wiki/Switzerland_in_the_Eurovision_Song_Contest_1988 "Switzerland in the Eurovision Song Contest 1988") with the song "[Ne partez pas sans moi](/wiki/Ne_partez_pas_sans_moi "Ne partez pas sans moi")". Dion went on to release [twelve English-language albums](/wiki/Celine_Dion_albums_discography "Celine Dion albums discography"), including the [best-selling albums](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_albums "List of best-selling albums") *[The Colour of My Love](/wiki/The_Colour_of_My_Love "The Colour of My Love")* (1993), *[Falling into You](/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")* (1996), and *[Let's Talk About Love](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love "Let's Talk About Love")* (1997), as well as *[A New Day Has Come](/wiki/A_New_Day_Has_Come "A New Day Has Come")* (2002), her [21st-century bestseller](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_albums_of_the_21st_century "List of best-selling albums of the 21st century"). She also became known for performing cinematic [theme songs](/wiki/Theme_music "Theme music"), such as "[Beauty and the Beast](/wiki/Beauty_and_the_Beast_(Disney_song) "Beauty and the Beast (Disney song)")" for the [Disney](/wiki/The_Walt_Disney_Company "The Walt Disney Company") [film of the same name](/wiki/Beauty_and_the_Beast_(1991_film) "Beauty and the Beast (1991 film)") (1991), "[Because You Loved Me](/wiki/Because_You_Loved_Me "Because You Loved Me")" for *[Up Close & Personal](/wiki/Up_Close_%26_Personal_(film) "Up Close & Personal (film)")* (1996), and "[My Heart Will Go On](/wiki/My_Heart_Will_Go_On "My Heart Will Go On")" for *[Titanic](/wiki/Titanic_(1997_film) "Titanic (1997 film)")* (1997), which became one of the [best-selling singles](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_singles "List of best-selling singles").

Dion continued releasing French-language albums between each English record, with *[D'eux](/wiki/D%27eux "D'eux")* (1995) becoming the best-selling French-language album. She also built her reputation as [a live performer](/wiki/List_of_Celine_Dion_concerts "List of Celine Dion concerts"), with the [Let's Talk About Love World Tour](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love_World_Tour "Let's Talk About Love World Tour") (1998–1999) and the [Taking Chances World Tour](/wiki/Taking_Chances_World_Tour "Taking Chances World Tour") (2008–2009)—which rank among the [highest-grossing concert tours](/wiki/List_of_highest-grossing_concert_tours "…", "truncated": true}}

{"action": "done", "result_summary": "Le briefing du mardi 9 juin 2026 est prêt : géopolitique (détroit d'Ormuz, Rafale en Lettonie, Gaza/Iran), santé (plan Ebola OMS/CDC Afrique), technologie (sommet IA à Lille, Choose France) et sport (Coupe du monde imminente).", "ui_payload": "# 📰 Briefing Matin — Mardi 9 juin 2026

---

## 🌍 Géopolitique & Sécurité

### Détroit d'Ormuz — Escalade Iran/États-Unis
Les tensions au détroit d'Ormuz atteignent un niveau critique. Les États-Unis ont abattu plusieurs drones iraniens qui menaçaient le trafic maritime dans ce passage stratégique. Téhéran avait préalablement lancé des salves de missiles et de drones contre ses voisins du Golfe et Israël. La situation reste explosive : l'uranium iranien serait introuvable selon plusieurs sources, alimentant les inquiétudes sur la prolifération nucléaire. Washington cherche à impliquer l'OTAN et Pékin dans la gestion de la crise.

### OTAN — Un Rafale français abat un drone en Lettonie
Le 8 juin 2026 (hier), un Rafale de l'armée de l'air française en mission OTAN a intercepté et abattu un drone « étranger » au-dessus du territoire letton. L'incident illustre la montée des tensions à la frontière orientale de l'Alliance atlantique.

### Ukraine — Impasse diplomatique
Zelensky a adressé un message à Poutine l'invitant à une rencontre directe. Moscou n'a pas répondu, signe de la persistance de l'impasse diplomatique.

### Gaza & Moyen-Orient
Les combats se poursuivent dans la bande de Gaza. Les frappes et contre-frappes entre Israël et l'Iran restent d'actualité, sans perspective d'accord de cessez-le-feu.

---

## 🏥 Santé mondiale

### Ébola — Plan d'urgence OMS & CDC Afrique
L'OMS et le CDC Afrique ont conjointement lancé un **plan de riposte d'urgence** contre l'épidémie d'Ébola qui sévit en **République Démocratique du Congo** et s'étend à l'**Ouganda**. Des agents de santé sont mobilisés sur le terrain à Bunia (province de l'Ituri). La situation est surveillée de près par la communauté internationale.

---

## 🇫🇷 France — Politique & Société

### Violences sexuelles en milieu scolaire
Le *Monde* rapporte une actualité sensible liée aux violences sexuelles dans les activités périscolaires, avec des révélations mettant en cause plusieurs structures d'accueil. Des enquêtes judiciaires seraient en cours.

### Sommet Choose France — Bilan
Le sommet *Choose France* du 1er juin a affiché un bilan dominé par les investissements dans l'**intelligence artificielle** (85 % des engagements fléchés vers l'IA et les data centers). Des voix critiques, dont *Libération*, soulignent que ces investissements ne freinent pas la désindustrialisation du pays et génèrent peu d'emplois directs.

---

## 💻 Technologie & IA

### Sommet européen de l'IA à Lille (12 juin)
La France organise le **12 juin 2026** à Lille (EuraTechnologies) un sommet européen de l'intelligence artificielle sous le thème « L'IA avec NOUS ». L'événement vise à poser les bases d'une gouvernance européenne de l'IA, en lien avec les travaux du G7 d'Évian où l'IA figure comme priorité absolue.

### IA trop « aimable » : un risque ?
Franceinfo publie une analyse sur les dérives des assistants IA qui, en cherchant à se rapprocher de l'humain, deviendraient de mauvais conseillers — privilégiant la validation sur la vérité. Un débat éthique qui prend de l'ampleur dans les cercles tech et académiques.

### Plan France 2030 — « Pionniers de l'IA »
Le programme gouvernemental *Pionniers de l'IA* (France 2030) continue de soutenir les acteurs porteurs d'innovations de rupture dans le domaine de l'IA, dans le cadre de la troisième phase de la stratégie nationale.

---

## ⚽ Sport — Coupe du Monde imminente
À environ une semaine du coup d'envoi de la **Coupe du Monde**, des tensions émergent aux États-Unis, pays hôte : des militants anti-Trump s'inquiètent du rôle que pourrait jouer l'agence ICE (immigration) lors de l'événement, ce qui pourrait affecter l'accueil des supporters étrangers.

---

## 📌 Résumé en un coup d'œil

| Thème | Point clé |
|---|---|
| 🔴 Géopolitique | Détroit d'Ormuz : drones iraniens abattus par les USA |
| 🔴 Défense | Rafale français abat un drone en Lettonie (OTAN) |
| 🟠 Santé | Plan Ébola lancé (OMS + CDC Afrique) en RDC/Ouganda |
| 🟡 France | Débat Choose France : IA vs emplois industriels |
| 🟢 Tech | Sommet IA à Lille le 12 juin ; débat sur l'IA « trop aimable » |
| 🟢 Sport | Coupe du Monde dans ~1 semaine, tensions politiques aux USA |

---

---

## 🌍 Contexte Macroéconomique

### 📈 Inflation en hausse des deux côtés de l'Atlantique
- **États-Unis** : l'inflation PCE (mesure privilégiée par la Fed) atteint **3,8% en avril 2026** (+0,9 point depuis février), portée par les prix des carburants (+29% sur un an). L'inflation cœur (Core PCE) s'établit à **+3,3%**, signalant des pressions persistantes.
- **Zone euro** : l'inflation remonte à **3,2% en mai 2026**, au-dessus de la cible BCE de 2%, principalement sous l'effet de la hausse des prix de l'énergie liée au conflit au Moyen-Orient.

### 🏦 Banques Centrales à la Croisée des Chemins
- La **BCE** avait maintenu ses taux inchangés en mars 2026, mais un **resserrement monétaire en juin** est désormais envisagé face à la remontée de l'inflation. Les marchés scrutent attentivement la prochaine réunion.
- La **Fed** fait face à un dilemme : l'inflation dépasse les objectifs, mais la croissance déçoit. Toute décision sur les taux sera scrutée par les investisseurs dans les prochaines semaines.

### 📉 Croissance Mondiale : Révision à la Baisse
- La croissance mondiale reste sous pression. Le conflit au Moyen-Orient crée des **risques à la baisse pour la croissance** et des **risques à la hausse pour l'inflation**, selon la BCE.
- L'Allemagne publie cette semaine des données industrielles importantes, indicateurs clés pour évaluer la santé économique de la zone euro.

---

## 🔥 Actualités Économiques et Géopolitiques Notables

### ✈️ Tensions au Moyen-Orient — Principal Facteur de Risque
- Les **frappes mutuelles entre Israël et l'Iran** ont rattrapé les espoirs de paix, pesant directement sur le prix du pétrole et la volatilité des marchés.
- Un **cessez-le-feu Israël-Liban** a néanmoins été annoncé, avec la reprise des discussions diplomatiques prévue la semaine du 22 juin — un signal légèrement positif.

### 🛢️ Pétrole Russe Saisi par la France
- La France a procédé à la **saisie du pétrolier russe Tagor**. Moscou a qualifié cet acte de *"piraterie"*, alimentant les tensions géopolitiques et les incertitudes sur les marchés de l'énergie.

### 🇺🇦 Conflit Russo-Ukrainien
- La Russie a mené une **attaque massive sur l'Ukraine**, faisant 11 morts et des dizaines de blessés, rappelant que le conflit reste une source de risque économique pour l'Europe.

### 📊 Agenda Macro de la Semaine
- **Chine & États-Unis** : publication des **balances commerciales** — focus particulier sur les flux pétroliers.
- **Allemagne** : publication de données industrielles clés pour évaluer la résilience de la première économie européenne.

---

# Discographie de Céline Dion

![Description de cette image, également commentée ci-après](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a3/Celine_Dion_en_concierto.jpg/250px-Celine_Dion_en_concierto.jpg)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [Albums studio](/wiki/Album_studio "Album studio")[↙](#Albums_studio) | 27 |
| [Albums live](/wiki/Album_live "Album live")[↙](#Albums_live) | 7 |
| [Compilations](/wiki/Compilation_(musique) "Compilation (musique)")[↙](#Compilations) | 19 |
| [Singles](/wiki/Single_(musique) "Single (musique)")[↙](#Singles) | 126 |
| [Musiques de film](/wiki/Musique_de_film "Musique de film")[↙](#Musiques_de_film) | 5 |

La **discographie de [Céline Dion](/wiki/C%C3%A9line_Dion "Céline Dion")** comprend l'ensemble des disques produits et publiés au cours de sa carrière. Elle se compose de 27 [albums studio](/wiki/Album_studio "Album studio") (dont 3 ayant pour thème les [chants de Noël](/wiki/Chant_de_No%C3%ABl "Chant de Noël")), 7 [albums en concert](/wiki/Albums_live "Albums live"), 14 [compilations](/wiki/Compilation_(musique) "Compilation (musique)") et près de 130 [singles](/wiki/Single_(musique) "Single (musique)"). Menant une carrière [francophone](/wiki/Francophonie "Francophonie") et [anglophone](/wiki/Anglais "Anglais"), la chanteuse [canadienne](/wiki/Canada "Canada") a publié 15 albums studio en [français](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ais "Français") (sa langue maternelle) et 12 en anglais.

Le premier album studio de Céline Dion, *[La Voix du Bon Dieu](/wiki/La_Voix_du_Bon_Dieu "La Voix du Bon Dieu")*, sort en [1981](/wiki/1981_en_musique "1981 en musique") et est distribué uniquement au [Québec](/wiki/Qu%C3%A9bec "Québec"). Son premier album en anglais, *[Unison](/wiki/Unison_(album) "Unison (album)")*, sort en [1990](/wiki/1990_en_musique "1990 en musique"). À partir des [années 1990](/wiki/Ann%C3%A9es_1990 "Années 1990"), elle alterne les albums et singles en français et en anglais, et parvient à s'imposer comme l'une des plus importantes vendeuses de disques au monde. Ses albums *[Falling into You](/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")* ([1996](/wiki/1996_en_musique "1996 en musique")) et *[Let's Talk About Love](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love_(album_de_C%C3%A9line_Dion) "Let's Talk About Love (album de Céline Dion)")* ([1997](/wiki/1997_en_musique "1997 en musique")) — sur lequel se trouve son plus grand succès commercial et radiophonique, *[My Heart Will Go On](/wiki/My_Heart_Will_Go_On "My Heart Will Go On")*, chanson du film *[Titanic](/wiki/Titanic_(film,_1997) "Titanic (film, 1997)")* sorti la même année — figurent notamment parmi [les plus vendus de l'histoire](/wiki/Liste_des_albums_musicaux_les_plus_vendus "Liste des albums musicaux les plus vendus"), tandis que *[D'eux](/wiki/D%27eux "D'eux")* ([1995](/wiki/1995_en_musique "1995 en musique")) demeure l'album francophone le plus vendu de tous les temps, en [France](/wiki/France "France") et dans le monde.

Les ventes de Céline Dion sont estimées à 230 millions de disques dans le monde[[1]](#cite_note-1),[[2]](#cite_note-2), dont plus de 25 millions en France[[3]](#cite_note-3).

# Discographie de Céline Dion

![Description de cette image, également commentée ci-après](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a3/Celine_Dion_en_concierto.jpg/250px-Celine_Dion_en_concierto.jpg)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [Albums studio](/wiki/Album_studio "Album studio")[↙](#Albums_studio) | 27 |
| [Albums live](/wiki/Album_live "Album live")[↙](#Albums_live) | 7 |
| [Compilations](/wiki/Compilation_(musique) "Compilation (musique)")[↙](#Compilations) | 19 |
| [Singles](/wiki/Single_(musique) "Single (musique)")[↙](#Singles) | 126 |
| [Musiques de film](/wiki/Musique_de_film "Musique de film")[↙](#Musiques_de_film) | 5 |

La **discographie de [Céline Dion](/wiki/C%C3%A9line_Dion "Céline Dion")** comprend l'ensemble des disques produits et publiés au cours de sa carrière. Elle se compose de 27 [albums studio](/wiki/Album_studio "Album studio") (dont 3 ayant pour thème les [chants de Noël](/wiki/Chant_de_No%C3%ABl "Chant de Noël")), 7 [albums en concert](/wiki/Albums_live "Albums live"), 14 [compilations](/wiki/Compilation_(musique) "Compilation (musique)") et près de 130 [singles](/wiki/Single_(musique) "Single (musique)"). Menant une carrière [francophone](/wiki/Francophonie "Francophonie") et [anglophone](/wiki/Anglais "Anglais"), la chanteuse [canadienne](/wiki/Canada "Canada") a publié 15 albums studio en [français](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ais "Français") (sa langue maternelle) et 12 en anglais.

Le premier album studio de Céline Dion, *[La Voix du Bon Dieu](/wiki/La_Voix_du_Bon_Dieu "La Voix du Bon Dieu")*, sort en [1981](/wiki/1981_en_musique "1981 en musique") et est distribué uniquement au [Québec](/wiki/Qu%C3%A9bec "Québec"). Son premier album en anglais, *[Unison](/wiki/Unison_(album) "Unison (album)")*, sort en [1990](/wiki/1990_en_musique "1990 en musique"). À partir des [années 1990](/wiki/Ann%C3%A9es_1990 "Années 1990"), elle alterne les albums et singles en français et en anglais, et parvient à s'imposer comme l'une des plus importantes vendeuses de disques au monde. Ses albums *[Falling into You](/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")* ([1996](/wiki/1996_en_musique "1996 en musique")) et *[Let's Talk About Love](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love_(album_de_C%C3%A9line_Dion) "Let's Talk About Love (album de Céline Dion)")* ([1997](/wiki/1997_en_musique "1997 en musique")) — sur lequel se trouve son plus grand succès commercial et radiophonique, *[My Heart Will Go On](/wiki/My_Heart_Will_Go_On "My Heart Will Go On")*, chanson du film *[Titanic](/wiki/Titanic_(film,_1997) "Titanic (film, 1997)")* sorti la même année — figurent notamment parmi [les plus vendus de l'histoire](/wiki/Liste_des_albums_musicaux_les_plus_vendus "Liste des albums musicaux les plus vendus"), tandis que *[D'eux](/wiki/D%27eux "D'eux")* ([1995](/wiki/1995_en_musique "1995 en musique")) demeure l'album francophone le plus vendu de tous les temps, en [France](/wiki/France "France") et dans le monde.

Les ventes de Céline Dion sont estimées à 230 millions de disques dans le monde[[1]](#cite_note-1),[[2]](#cite_note-2), dont plus de 25 millions en France[[3]](#cite_note-3).

# Celine Dion

**Céline Marie Claudette Dion**[[a]](#cite_note-3) (born 30 March 1968) is a Canadian singer, entrepreneur and philanthropist.[[3]](#cite_note-4)[[4]](#cite_note-5) Dubbed the "[Queen of Power Ballads](/wiki/Honorific_nicknames_in_popular_music "Honorific nicknames in popular music")", she is known for her powerful, technically skilled vocals and commercially successful works, which have had a [significant impact on popular music](/wiki/Cultural_impact_of_Celine_Dion "Cultural impact of Celine Dion"). With over 200 million records sold worldwide, Dion is the best-selling Canadian recording artist, the best-selling French-language artist, and one of the [best-selling musical artists](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_music_artists "List of best-selling music artists").

Born into a large family in [Charlemagne, Quebec](/wiki/Charlemagne,_Quebec "Charlemagne, Quebec"), Dion was discovered by her future manager and husband, [René Angélil](/wiki/Ren%C3%A9_Ang%C3%A9lil "René Angélil"), and emerged as a teen star in her home country with eight French-language albums during the 1980s. She gained international recognition by winning the [Eurovision Song Contest 1988](/wiki/Eurovision_Song_Contest_1988 "Eurovision Song Contest 1988"), where she [represented Switzerland](/wiki/Switzerland_in_the_Eurovision_Song_Contest_1988 "Switzerland in the Eurovision Song Contest 1988") with the song "[Ne partez pas sans moi](/wiki/Ne_partez_pas_sans_moi "Ne partez pas sans moi")". Dion went on to release [twelve English-language albums](/wiki/Celine_Dion_albums_discography "Celine Dion albums discography"), including the [best-selling albums](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_albums "List of best-selling albums") *[The Colour of My Love](/wiki/The_Colour_of_My_Love "The Colour of My Love")* (1993), *[Falling into You](/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")* (1996), and *[Let's Talk About Love](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love "Let's Talk About Love")* (1997), as well as *[A New Day Has Come](/wiki/A_New_Day_Has_Come "A New Day Has Come")* (2002), her [21st-century bestseller](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_albums_of_the_21st_century "List of best-selling albums of the 21st century"). She also became known for performing cinematic [theme songs](/wiki/Theme_music "Theme music"), such as "[Beauty and the Beast](/wiki/Beauty_and_the_Beast_(Disney_song) "Beauty and the Beast (Disney song)")" for the [Disney](/wiki/The_Walt_Disney_Company "The Walt Disney Company") [film of the same name](/wiki/Beauty_and_the_Beast_(1991_film) "Beauty and the Beast (1991 film)") (1991), "[Because You Loved Me](/wiki/Because_You_Loved_Me "Because You Loved Me")" for *[Up Close & Personal](/wiki/Up_Close_%26_Personal_(film) "Up Close & Personal (film)")* (1996), and "[My Heart Will Go On](/wiki/My_Heart_Will_Go_On "My Heart Will Go On")" for *[Titanic](/wiki/Titanic_(1997_film) "Titanic (1997 film)")* (1997), which became one of the [best-selling singles](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_singles "List of best-selling singles").

Dion continued releasing French-language albums between each English record, with *[D'eux](/wiki/D%27eux "D'eux")* (1995) becoming the best-selling French-language album. She also built her reputation as [a live performer](/wiki/List_of_Celine_Dion_concerts "List of Celine Dion concerts"), with the [Let's Talk About Love World Tour](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love_World_Tour "Let's Talk About Love World Tour") (1998–1999) and the [Taking Chances World Tour](/wiki/Taking_Chances_World_Tour "Taking Chances World Tour") (2008–2009)—which rank among the [highest-grossing concert tours](/wiki/List_of_highest-grossing_concert_tours "…", "truncated": true}}

# Celine Dion albums discography

![This is a featured list. Click here for more information.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/e/e7/Cscr-featured.svg/20px-Cscr-featured.svg.png)

| [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") albums discography | |
| --- | --- |
| Dion performing on the [Taking Chances World Tour](/wiki/Taking_Chances_World_Tour "Taking Chances World Tour") in August 2008 | |
| Studio albums | 27 |
| Soundtrack albums | 2 |
| Live albums | 7 |
| Compilation albums | 20 |

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/42/Celine_Dion_Concert_Singing_Taking_Chances_2008.jpg/250px-Celine_Dion_Concert_Singing_Taking_Chances_2008.jpg)

Canadian singer [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") has released 27 [studio albums](/wiki/Album#Studio "Album"), 7 [live albums](/wiki/Album#Live "Album"), 20 [compilation albums](/wiki/Compilation_album "Compilation album"), and 25 [box sets](/wiki/Box_set "Box set"). Often described as the "Queen of power ballads",[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Dion is the best-selling Canadian artist of all time and the best-selling artist of the [Nielsen SoundScan](/wiki/Nielsen_SoundScan "Nielsen SoundScan") era in Canada (from 1996 to the present).[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) She is also the second best-selling female artist of the Nielsen era in the United States, with over 53.2 million albums sold since Nielsen began tracking sales in 1991.[[6]](#cite_note-6) *[Guinness World Records](/wiki/Guinness_World_Records "Guinness World Records")* recognises her as the Top Selling Album Act in Europe, with 33 million certified album sales since 1996.[[7]](#cite_note-7) In 2003, Dion was honoured by the [International Federation of the Phonographic Industry](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") for selling 50 million albums in Europe.[[8]](#cite_note-8) *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* ranked her the second best-selling female album artist of the 2000s in the United States, with 17.3 million albums sold via SoundScan.[[9]](#cite_note-9) According to *Billboard*, Dion is the sixth greatest [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") female solo artist of all time,[[10]](#cite_note-10) as well as the eighth greatest female solo artist of all time in *Billboard'*s overall chart history.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Her debut album, *[La voix du bon Dieu](/wiki/La_voix_du_bon_Dieu "La voix du bon Dieu")*, was issued in 1981. In the 1980s, Dion released her French-language albums in Canada, with several compilation albums also issued in France. Her first English-language album, *[Unison](/wiki/Unison_(Celine_Dion_album) "Unison (Celine Dion album)")*, was released in 1990 and has sold over four million copies worldwide.[[12]](#cite_note-unison-12) It was followed by *[Dion chante Plamondon](/wiki/Dion_chante_Plamondon "Dion chante Plamondon")* in 1991 and *[Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion_(album) "Celine Dion (album)")* in 1992. The latter became one of six of her albums to be certified [Diamond](/wiki/Music_Canada#Certification_awards "Music Canada") in Canada for shipments of at least one million units.[[13]](#cite_note-cria-13) Dion's popularity became firmly established with her 1993 album, *[The Colour of My Love](/wiki/The_Colour_of_My_Love "The Colour of My Love")*, which topped charts in several countries, including the United Kingdom, Canada, and Australia, and has sold 20 million copies worldwide.[[14]](#cite_note-thecolourofmylove-14) In the United States, it was certified [six-times platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification").[[15]](#cite_note-riaa-15) Released in 1995, *[D'eux](/wiki/D%27eux "D'eux")* became the best-selling French-language album in history, with sales of 12 million copies worldwide.[[16]](#cite_note-elle.fr-16)[[17]](#cite_note-deux-17) In France alone, *D'eux* spent 44 weeks at number one and has sold 4.5 million units, becoming the best-selling album of all time.[[18]](#cite_note-10million-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19) It also became Dion's first of six [Diamond](/wiki/Syndicat_National_de_l%27%C3%89dition_Phonographique#Certification_awards "Syndicat National de l'Édition Phonographique")-certified albums in France.[[20]](#cite_note-snep-20)

*[Falling into You](/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")* (1996) and *[Let's Talk About Love](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love "Let's Talk About Love")* (1997) were major successes for Dion, both reaching number one in numerous countries and becoming two of the [best-selling albums of all time](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_albums "List of best-selling albums"), with sales of over 32 million and 31 million copies, respectively.[[21]](#cite_note-fallingintoyou-21)[[22]](#cite_note-letstalkaboutlove-22) Both albums were certified [Diamond](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") in the United States, with *Falling into You* later certified [twelve-times platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") and *Let's Talk About Love* certified eleven-times platinum for 11 million units.[[15]](#cite_note-riaa-15) *Let's Talk About Love* became the first album to be certified [ten-times platinum](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry#Record_sales_certification "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") by the [IFPI](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") for sales of over 10 million copies in Europe.[[23]](#cite_note-europeancerts-23) In 1998, Dion released another French-language album, *[S'il suffisait d'aimer](/wiki/S%27il_suffisait_d%27aimer "S'il suffisait d'aimer")*, and her …", "truncated": true}}

Canadian singer [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") has released 27 [studio albums](/wiki/Album#Studio "Album"), 2 [soundtrack albums](/wiki/Soundtrack_album "Soundtrack album"), 7 [live albums](/wiki/Album#Live "Album"), 20 [compilation albums](/wiki/Compilation_album "Compilation album"), and 25 [box sets](/wiki/Box_set "Box set"). Often described as the "Queen of power ballads",[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Dion is the best-selling Canadian artist of all time and the best-selling artist of the [Nielsen SoundScan](/wiki/Nielsen_SoundScan "Nielsen SoundScan") era in Canada (from 1996 to the present).[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) She is also the second best-selling female artist of the Nielsen era in the United States, with over 53.2 million albums sold since Nielsen began tracking sales in 1991.[[6]](#cite_note-6) *[Guinness World Records](/wiki/Guinness_World_Records "Guinness World Records")* recognises her as the Top Selling Album Act in Europe, with 33 million certified album sales since 1996.[[7]](#cite_note-7) In 2003, Dion was honoured by the [International Federation of the Phonographic Industry](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") for selling 50 million albums in Europe.[[8]](#cite_note-8) *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* ranked her the second best-selling female album artist of the 2000s in the United States, with 17.3 million albums sold via SoundScan.[[9]](#cite_note-9) According to *Billboard*, Dion is the sixth greatest [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") female solo artist of all time,[[10]](#cite_note-10) as well as the eighth greatest female solo artist of all time in *Billboard'*s overall chart history.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Her debut album, *[La voix du bon Dieu](/wiki/La_voix_du_bon_Dieu "La voix du bon Dieu")*, was issued in 1981. In the 1980s, Dion released her French-language albums in Canada, with several compilation albums also issued in France. Her first English-language album, *[Unison](/wiki/Unison_(Celine_Dion_album) "Unison (Celine Dion album)")*, was released in 1990 and has sold over four million copies worldwide.[[12]](#cite_note-unison-12) It was followed by *[Dion chante Plamondon](/wiki/Dion_chante_Plamondon "Dion chante Plamondon")* in 1991 and *[Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion_(album) "Celine Dion (album)")* in 1992. The latter became one of six of her albums to be certified [Diamond](/wiki/Music_Canada#Certification_awards "Music Canada") in Canada for shipments of at least one million units.[[13]](#cite_note-cria-13) Dion's popularity became firmly established with her 1993 album, *[The Colour of My Love](/wiki/The_Colour_of_My_Love "The Colour of My Love")*, which topped charts in several countries, including the United Kingdom, Canada, and Australia, and has sold 20 million copies worldwide.[[14]](#cite_note-thecolourofmylove-14) In the United States, it was certified [six-times platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification").[[15]](#cite_note-riaa-15) Released in 1995, *[D'eux](/wiki/D%27eux "D'eux")* became the best-selling French-language album in history, with sales of 12 million copies worldwide.[[16]](#cite_note-elle.fr-16)[[17]](#cite_note-deux-17) In France alone, *D'eux* spent 44 weeks at number one and has sold 4.5 million units, becoming the best-selling album of all time.[[18]](#cite_note-10million-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19) It also became Dion's first of six [Diamond](/wiki/Syndicat_National_de_l%27%C3%89dition_Phonographique#Certification_awards "Syndicat National de l'Édition Phonographique")-certified albums in France.[[20]](#cite_note-snep-20)

*[Falling into You](/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")* (1996) and *[Let's Talk About Love](/wiki/Let%27s_Talk_About_Love "Let's Talk About Love")* (1997) were major successes for Dion, both reaching number one in numerous countries and becoming two of the [best-selling albums of all time](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_albums "List of best-selling albums"), with sales of over 32 million and 31 million copies, respectively.[[21]](#cite_note-fallingintoyou-21)[[22]](#cite_note-letstalkaboutlove-22) Both albums were certified [Diamond](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") in the United States, with *Falling into You* later certified [twelve-times platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") and *Let's Talk About Love* certified eleven-times platinum for 11 million units.[[15]](#cite_note-riaa-15) *Let's Talk About Love* became the first album to be certified [ten-times platinum](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry#Record_sales_certification "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") by the [IFPI](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") for sales of over 10 million copies in Europe.[[23]](#cite_note-europeancerts-23) In 1998, Dion released another French-language album, *[S'il suff…", "truncated": true}}

| *[The Best So Far... 2018 Tour Edition](/wiki/The_Best_So_Far..._2018_Tour_Edition "The Best So Far... 2018 Tour Edition")* (2018) | ***Courage*** (2019) | *[Love Again](/wiki/Love_Again_(soundtrack) "Love Again (soundtrack)")* (2023) |

***Courage*** is the twenty-seventh studio album and twelfth English-language album by Canadian singer [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion"). Released by [Columbia Records](/wiki/Columbia_Records "Columbia Records") on 15 November 2019, it was created with a diverse group of writers and producers, including [Sia](/wiki/Sia "Sia"), [Sam Smith](/wiki/Sam_Smith "Sam Smith"), [David Guetta](/wiki/David_Guetta "David Guetta"), [Lauv](/wiki/Lauv "Lauv"), [LP](/wiki/LP_(singer) "LP (singer)"), [Greg Kurstin](/wiki/Greg_Kurstin "Greg Kurstin"), [StarGate](/wiki/Stargate_(production_team) "Stargate (production team)"), [Jimmy Napes](/wiki/Jimmy_Napes "Jimmy Napes"), [Jörgen Elofsson](/wiki/J%C3%B6rgen_Elofsson "Jörgen Elofsson"), [Stephan Moccio](/wiki/Stephan_Moccio "Stephan Moccio"), [Eg White](/wiki/Eg_White "Eg White"), and Liz Rodrigues of [The New Royales](/wiki/The_New_Royales "The New Royales").

The album marked Dion's return to the studio following the end of her Las Vegas residency, [Celine](/wiki/Celine_(concert_residency) "Celine (concert residency)"), in June 2019. She released "[Flying on My Own](/wiki/Flying_on_My_Own "Flying on My Own")" as a gift to fans shortly afterward, and in September 2019 launched the [Courage World Tour](/wiki/Courage_World_Tour "Courage World Tour"), where she premiered three additional songs: "[Imperfections](/wiki/Imperfections_(song) "Imperfections (song)")", "[Lying Down](/wiki/Lying_Down "Lying Down")", and "[Courage](/wiki/Courage_(Celine_Dion_song) "Courage (Celine Dion song)")". *Courage* includes 16 tracks, with four more on the deluxe edition, and received generally favourable reviews from music critics.

The album debuted atop the US [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), becoming Dion's first number-one album there in 17 years. It also entered at number one in Canada, Belgium, and Switzerland, and reached the top 10 in numerous other countries, including number two in the United Kingdom, France, and Australia, number four in Germany, Austria, and Croatia, and number five in Ireland, New Zealand, and Portugal.

## Background and development

In November 2013, Dion released her previous English-language studio album, *[Loved Me Back to Life](/wiki/Loved_Me_Back_to_Life "Loved Me Back to Life")*.[[1]](#cite_note…", "truncated": true}}

# *Love Again* (soundtrack)

***Love Again (Soundtrack from the Motion Picture)*** is the soundtrack album to the 2023 American romantic comedy‑drama film *[Love Again](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Love_Again_(2023_film) "Love Again (2023 film)")*, directed by [James C. Strouse](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_C._Strouse "James C. Strouse"). It was released by [Columbia Records](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Columbia_Records "Columbia Records") on 12 May 2023. The album includes five new songs by [Celine Dion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") alongside six of her earlier hits. The lead single, "[Love Again](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Love_Again_(Celine_Dion_song) "Love Again (Celine Dion song)")", was issued on 13 April 2023, followed by "[I'll Be](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/I'll_Be_(Celine_Dion_song) "I'll Be (Celine Dion song)")" on 5 May 2023. Other new tracks are "Waiting on You", "Love of My Life", and "[The Gift](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Gift_(Celine_Dion_song) "The Gift (Celine Dion song)")". *Love Again* received positive reviews from music critics and debuted at number one in Quebec, number two on the UK Soundtrack Albums chart, number six in Switzerland's Romandy region and number 10 in Belgium's Wallonia.

## Contents

## Development

In October 2020, [Priyanka Chopra](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Priyanka_Chopra "Priyanka Chopra"), [Sam Heughan](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sam_Heughan "Sam Heughan"), and [Celine Dion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") joined the cast of the film, then tentatively titled *Text for You*.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Principal photography began in October 2020 and concluded in early 2021.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In April 2022, it was announced that the film had been retitled *It's All Coming Back to Me*, after the song "[It's All Coming Back to Me Now](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/It's_All_Coming_Back_to_Me_Now#Celine_Dion_version "It's All Coming Back to Me Now")", which Dion recorded for her 1996 album *[Falling into You](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Falling_into_You "Falling into You")*.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In November 2022, the title was changed again to *Love Again*.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The film was released theatrically on 5 May 2023.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Songs

*Love Again* includes 11 songs by [Celine Dion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") that are woven into the narrative of the film and its characters.[[6]](#cite_note-hollywoodreporter-6) The album contains five new Dion songs and six of her past hits, among them a new edit of "[It's All Coming Back to Me Now](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/It's_All_Coming_Back_to_Me_Now#Celine_Dion_version "It's All Coming Back to Me Now")", "[All by Myself](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/All_by_Myself#Celine_Dion_version "All by Myself")", "[That's the Way It Is](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/That's_the_Way_It_Is_(Celine_Dion_song) "That's the Way It Is (Celine Dion song)")", "[Where Does My Heart Beat Now](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Where_Does_My_Heart_Beat_Now "Where Does My Heart Beat Now")", "[Courage](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Courage_(Celine_Dion_song) "Courage (Celine Dion song)")", and the album version of "[A New Day Has Come](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/A_New_Day_Has_Come_(song) "A New Day Has Come (song)")". The soundtrack also includes three score selections written and produced by American film composer [Keegan DeWitt](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Keegan_DeWitt "Keegan DeWitt").[[6]](#cite_note-hollywoodreporter-6)

# *I Am: Celine Dion* (soundtrack)

***I Am: Celine Dion*** is the soundtrack album to the 2024 documentary film *[I Am: Celine Dion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/I_Am:_Celine_Dion "I Am: Celine Dion")*, directed by [Irene Taylor](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Irene_Taylor_(filmmaker) "Irene Taylor (filmmaker)"), which focuses on Canadian singer [Celine Dion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion"). It was released by [Columbia Records](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Columbia_Records "Columbia Records") on 21 June 2024. The album contains 13 of Dion's well‑known songs, along with seven original score tracks composed mainly by [Redi Hasa](https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Redi%20Hasa "it:Redi Hasa") and produced by Alberto Fabris. In Francophone markets, it was issued under the title ***Je suis: Céline Dion***. The album topped the chart in Quebec for three weeks and reached the top 10 on compilation charts in the United Kingdom and Ireland.
